action.content:
Name geometric shapes from their SVG paths.

Q: This SVG path element <path d="M 31.00,73.00 L 32.00,59.00 L 44.00,50.00 L 49.00,41.00 L 64.00,37.00 L 71.00,55.00 L 64.00,76.00 L 52.00,61.00 L 31.00,73.00"/> draws a
Options:
(A) circle
(B) heptagon
(C) hexagon
(D) kite
(E) line
(F) octagon
(G) pentagon
(H) rectangle
(I) sector
(J) triangle
A: (F)

Q: This SVG path element <path d="M 14.19,26.04 L 51.43,39.21 L 58.44,36.69 L 56.63,30.17 L 48.53,26.66 L 14.19,26.04"/> draws a
Options:
(A) circle
(B) heptagon
(C) hexagon
(D) kite
(E) line
(F) octagon
(G) pentagon
(H) rectangle
(I) sector
(J) triangle
A: (G)

Q: This SVG path element <path d="M 41.00,43.00 L 37.00,34.00 L 41.00,33.00 L 45.00,34.00 L 41.00,43.00"/> draws a
Options:
(A) circle
(B) heptagon
(C) hexagon
(D) kite
(E) line
(F) octagon
(G) pentagon
(H) rectangle
(I) sector
(J) triangle
A: (D)

Q: This SVG path element <path d="M 31.14,30.62 L 27.61,16.81 L 36.59,14.51 L 44.38,16.20 L 49.10,26.03 L 38.64,42.68 L 22.16,32.91 L 31.14,30.62"/> draws a
Options:
(A) circle
(B) heptagon
(C) hexagon
(D) kite
(E) line
(F) octagon
(G) pentagon
(H) rectangle
(I) sector
(J) triangle
A:
action.label: context

action.content:
To determine the geometric shape, let's count the number of distinct vertices (corners) defined by the `L` (line to) commands in the path before the path closes on the starting point.

The path is:
`M 31.14,30.62` (Start point)
`L 27.61,16.81` (Vertex 1)
`L 36.59,14.51` (Vertex 2)
`L 44.38,16.20` (Vertex 3)
`L 49.10,26.03` (Vertex 4)
`L 38.64,42.68` (Vertex 5)
`L 22.16,32.91` (Vertex 6)
`L 31.14,30.62` (Back to Start point)

Counting the segments between distinct points:
1. Start $\to$ Vertex 1
2. Vertex 1 $\to$ Vertex 2
3. Vertex 2 $\to$ Vertex 3
4. Vertex 3 $\to$ Vertex 4
5. Vertex 4 $\to$ Vertex 5
6. Vertex 5 $\to$ Vertex 6
7. Vertex 6 $\to$ Start (This closes the loop)

There are **6** distinct vertices forming a closed loop with 6 sides. A polygon with 6 sides is called a **hexagon**.

Looking at the options:
(A) circle
(B) heptagon (7 sides)
(C) hexagon (6 sides)
(D) kite (4 sides, usually with axis symmetry)
(E) line
(F) octagon (8 sides)
(G) pentagon (5 sides)
(H) rectangle (4 sides)
(I) sector
(J) triangle (3 sides)

The correct classification is a hexagon.

A: (C)
action.label: response
